description_long: This is a rectangular outdoor tennis court featuring a blue playing surface with white line markings for singles and doubles play. The court is surrounded by a low, transparent fence with red posts, providing a clear boundary while maintaining visibility. At one end, there is a large, modern scoreboard structure with digital displays and a central opening, flanked by two pole-mounted lighting fixtures on each side. The net is suspended between two sturdy posts and appears to be made of braided material. The overall design is functional and contemporary, with a focus on utility and clear sightlines for both players and spectators. The court is elevated on a platform, suggesting it is part of a larger sports complex or urban recreational area.
description: A modern blue tennis court with white markings, transparent fencing, a large digital scoreboard, and pole-mounted lighting fixtures. The court is elevated and designed for both singles and doubles play.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue court, white net, transparent fencing, and the scoreboard structure at the far end.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the large digital scoreboard, central opening, and the full width of the court with lighting fixtures visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, emphasizing the net, fencing, and the scoreboard's side profile.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the scoreboard structure, with the court and fencing less visible.
attribute_net_type: Braided nylon net, white, supported by two metal posts with visible tensioning mechanisms.
attribute_court_line_markings: All standard tennis lines present, white, approximately 5 cm wide, clearly visible and complete.
attribute_surrounding_fence: Transparent mesh or glass fence with red posts, approximately 2.5 meters high, covering the full perimeter.
attribute_lighting_fixtures: Four pole-mounted lighting fixtures, two on each side of the scoreboard end, modern design.
attribute_architectural_style: Modern utilitarian with clean lines, functional structures, and minimal decorative elements.
